core_claim: SBERT fine-tunes BERT with siamese and triplet network structures to produce sentence embeddings that outperform state-of-the-art sentence embedding methods (InferSent, Universal Sentence Encoder) on STS tasks by 11.7 and 5.5 Spearman correlation points respectively, while reducing pairwise similarity search over 10,000 sentences from 65 hours to about 5 seconds.

datasets.name: SNLI
datasets.preprocessing: Annotated with labels: contradiction, entailment, neutral. Used with 3-way softmax classification objective.

datasets.name: Multi-Genre NLI (MultiNLI)
datasets.preprocessing: Covers a range of genres of spoken and written text. Combined with SNLI for training.

datasets.name: STSbenchmark (STSb)
datasets.preprocessing: Three categories: captions, news, forums. Labels 0-5 on semantic relatedness.

datasets.name: Argument Facet Similarity (AFS)
datasets.preprocessing: Three topics: gun control, gay marriage, death penalty. Annotated 0-5 scale.

datasets.name: Wikipedia Sections Distinction
datasets.preprocessing: Anchor and positive from same Wikipedia section, negative from different section of same article.

datasets.name: SentEval (MR, CR, SUBJ, MPQA, SST, TREC, MRPC)
datasets.preprocessing: Sentence embeddings used as features for logistic regression classifier with 10-fold cross-validation.

model.architecture: BERT (base or large) with siamese/triplet network structure. Adds pooling operation (default: MEAN) on top of BERT output to derive fixed-size sentence embeddings. For classification objective, concatenates sentence embeddings u and v with element-wise difference |u-v| and multiplies with trainable weight W_t for softmax classification. For regression objective, computes cosine similarity between embeddings with MSE loss.

training.lr_schedule: Linear warmup over 10% of training data

metrics.name: Spearman correlation (rho x 100)
metrics.conditions: Unsupervised STS, cosine similarity, BERT-base fine-tuned on NLI

metrics.name: Spearman correlation (rho x 100)
metrics.conditions: Unsupervised STS, cosine similarity, BERT-large fine-tuned on NLI

metrics.name: Spearman correlation (rho x 100)
metrics.conditions: Supervised STS, first trained on NLI then STSb, cosine similarity, 10 random seeds, +/- 0.17

metrics.name: Spearman correlation (rho x 100)
metrics.conditions: Supervised STS, first trained on NLI then STSb, cosine similarity, 10 random seeds, +/- 0.13

metrics.name: Spearman correlation (rho x 100)
metrics.conditions: Supervised STS, first trained on NLI then STSb, cosine similarity, 10 random seeds, +/- 0.35

metrics.name: SentEval average accuracy
metrics.dataset: SentEval (MR, CR, SUBJ, MPQA, SST, TREC, MRPC)
metrics.conditions: Logistic regression on sentence embeddings, 10-fold cross-validation

metrics.name: SentEval average accuracy
metrics.dataset: SentEval (MR, CR, SUBJ, MPQA, SST, TREC, MRPC)
metrics.conditions: Logistic regression on sentence embeddings, 10-fold cross-validation

metrics.name: Accuracy
metrics.conditions: Triplet objective, 1 epoch training on ~1.8M triplets

ablations.name: pooling_strategy
ablations.description: Compares MEAN, MAX, and CLS pooling strategies. With classification objective on NLI data, pooling has minor impact. With regression objective, MAX performs significantly worse than MEAN or CLS.

ablations.name: concatenation_mode
ablations.description: Compares different ways to combine sentence embeddings u and v for the classification objective: (|u-v|), (u*v), (|u-v|, u*v), (u, v, u*v), (u, v, |u-v|), (u, v, |u-v|, u*v). The element-wise difference |u-v| is the most important component. (u, v, |u-v|) achieves 80.78 on STSb dev, outperforming the (u, v, |u-v|, u*v) variant used by InferSent and USE.

checkpoints.name: sentence-transformers/bert-base-nli-mean-tokens
checkpoints.what_it_is: SBERT-NLI-base: BERT-base fine-tuned on SNLI+MultiNLI with MEAN pooling, released via sentence-transformers library on HuggingFace

checkpoints.name: sentence-transformers/bert-large-nli-mean-tokens
checkpoints.what_it_is: SBERT-NLI-large: BERT-large fine-tuned on SNLI+MultiNLI with MEAN pooling, released via sentence-transformers library on HuggingFace

reproducibility_notes: Authors report supervised STS results averaged over 10 random seeds with standard deviations. Training takes less than 20 minutes. The sentence-transformers library provides pre-trained models on HuggingFace Hub for direct evaluation.
compute_feasibility_note: Pre-trained SBERT checkpoints are available on HuggingFace (sentence-transformers library), making evaluation-only reproduction CPU-feasible in minutes — BERT-base requires ~440MB in FP32 and STS evaluation datasets are small. Even full training from scratch is feasible on a single GPU in under 20 minutes as stated by the authors.
